session.user: Run the following command from the repository root /home/runner/work/erk/erk and report all results: make fast-ci Report any failures in detail.
session.assistant: I'll run the `make fast-ci` command from the repository root and report the results.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Run make fast-ci from repository root

session.tool_result:
Exit code 2 [[33mwarn[39m] docs/learned/integrations/github-review-decision.md [[33mwarn[39m] docs/learned/tui/status-indicators.md [[33mwarn[39m] Code style issues found in 2 files. Run Prettier with --write to fix. ✓ AGENTS.md standard: PASSED
All CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md files properly paired.
CLAUDE.md files checked: 19 Violations: 0 make: *** [Makefile:97: fast-ci] Error 1

--- Lint --- All checks passed!
--- Format Check --- 1636 files already formatted

--- Markdown Check ---
--- Exec Reference Check --- OK: .claude/skills/erk-exec/reference.md is up to date

... [104 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant:
The `make fast-ci` command failed due to formatting issues. Here's the summary:
## Results Summary
**Status: FAILED (Exit Code 2)**
### Failures Found
**Prettier Formatting Issues (2 files):** 1. `docs/learned/integrations/github-review-decision.md` 2. `docs/learned/tui/status-indicators.md`
These files have code style issues and need to be formatted with Prettier.
### Successful Checks
The following checks passed: - **Lint**: All checks passed - **Format Check**: 1636 files already formatted - **Markdown Check**: Passed - **Exec Reference Check**: OK - **Type Checking (ty)**: All checks passed - **Unit Tests (erk)**: 5641 passed - **Unit Tests (erk-dev)**: 136 passed - **Unit Tests (erk-statusline)**: 115 passed - **Unit Tests (erk-mcp)**: 24 passed
### Details
The make fast-ci pipeline failed at the Prettier check step. Two markdown documentation files in the `docs/learned/` directory have formatting issues that don't meet the Prettier code style standard. The test suite itself executed successfully with all 5916 total tests passing across all packages.
